textSpans.text: Southern Hills Hospital and Medical Center (COCSNV)
Patient is a 78-year-old female patient with a past medical history of vertigo, chronic balance
Patient reports she has had more than 12 falls in the last several months.
Patient reports she had her similar chronic balance
Reconcile home medications and continue: Patient does not take any home meds
Patient seen and evaluated at bedside.
On day of discharge, patient had stable labs and vitals.
Pt disposition to home w/ home PT, patient in agreement.
Pt Pt was encouraged to see Neurology inpatient due to her hx of
enlarged ventricles and gait dysfunction, but patient refused, but said she would consider
Pt sent with referral and instructed to follow up with Desert
Discharge to: Home Health wPlan of Care
Additional Discharge Routines: PCP Follow-Up, Consultant Follow-Up
Does not report any chest pain
She reports she did not hit her head.
ENT: moist mucosal membranes, normal ear left
Neuro/CNS: alert, oriented X 3, normal speech
Psychiatry: normal affect, normal judgment/insight, normal mood
Oxygen as needed to maintain saturations above 92%
Cardiovascular: normal heart sounds, regular rate & rhythm
Respiratory: aerating well, clear to auscultation
History of balance issues with multiple falls
days, multiple falls and incontinence who presents to the emergency department today after
a fall that happened shortly prior to arrival.
palpitations or chest pain prior to fall.
issues that cause her to fall.
a fall that happened shortly prior to arrival. palpitations or chest pain prior to fall. issues that cause her to fall. No LOC or sudden weakness. she reports she had a

reports that today she was walking around using her walker outside in her garden and fell
No LOC or sudden weakness. she reports she had a
glass of white wine when she was walking outside.
Musculoskeletal: Left lower extremity is shortened and slightly externally rotated.

She presents emergency department today via
She has been dealing with the balance issues for last several
NPO at midnight, pending left hip surgery tomorrow
Plan for left hip cephalomedullary nail
postoperatively need placement in rehab versus SNF.
Pt seen and evaluated at bedside.
Pt Left hip cephulmedullary nail surgery completed.
Pt recieved dose of vancomycin post-op.
Pt sent out on home meds and aspirin 81mg BID for 6
Pt to f/u with Ortho Surg in
following up in the outpt setting.
Answered any questinos or concerns appropriately.
Start taking the following new medications:
Neck: full range of motion, non-tender
Extremities: moves all, normal capillary refill
PCP follow up timeframe: In 4 days
Consult follow up timeframe: In 1-2 weeks
Electronically Signed by GHASHAM, HUSSEIN DO R1 on 04/01/26 at 1153

chartSnapshot.primary_reason_for_home_health_medical_n: Mary Treatman, a 78-year-old female, is being admitted to home health care following her recent hospitalization for a left intertrochanteric femur fracture sustained from a fall. Her primary diagnosis is a displaced left intertrochanteric femur fracture, which required surgical intervention in the form of internal fixation using a cephalomedullary nail. Secondary conditions include a history of vertigo, chronic balance issues, and frequent falls, with over a dozen episodes in the past several months, significantly
chartSnapshot.homebound_narrative: Patient is homebound due to her surgery, and she exhibited significant functional limitations, including non-weight-bearing restrictions on her left leg, considerable pain with movement, and reliance on mobility aids such as a cane and walker. Given her advanced age and chronic health issues, including moderate bilateral hip osteoarthritis, she is at heightened risk for complications and requires ongoing skilled care for recovery.
